name: GTandDiedTooSoons OpenAI Production Orchestration
purpose: A repeatable, auditable orchestration contract for AI-assisted Ableton Live 12 Suite, Max for Live, source-provenance, and account-authorized library operations.
operating_assumptions: AI workers can propose musical, technical, and operational changes, but material changes must be traceable to versioned manifests or human-approved local sessions.

DAW and account automation is in scope when it uses official user-authenticated surfaces, redacts sensitive data, and honors confirmation gates for purchases, installs, Live-set mutation, export, and release.
Repository CI must verify orchestration contracts without requiring OpenAI credentials, vendor credentials, Ableton Live, Max for Live, Arturia Software Center, or private audio.
Generated prompts, briefs, and manifests must avoid raw private audio, unreleased lyrics, credentials, account tokens, installer payloads, and commercial library content.

api_surfaces.name: Responses API
api_surfaces.use: Generate structured arrangement proposals, worker briefs, source summaries, session patch plans, and release checklists from repository manifests.
guardrails: Use structured outputs for machine-consumable plans.
Keep prompts metadata-focused unless a human explicitly approves private creative content.
Store only durable outputs that are useful for review, repeatability, or release audit.

api_surfaces.name: Agents SDK
api_surfaces.use: Coordinate role-specific production agents with handoffs, tool contracts, trace review, and approval boundaries.
guardrails: Agents must receive role-scoped instructions and narrow tools.
Handoffs must preserve provenance and unresolved risk notes.
Trace logs must redact secrets, credentials, account pages, and private audio content.

api_surfaces.name: Realtime API
api_surfaces.use: Optional low-latency rehearsal talkback, live critique, controller coaching, and spoken checklist interaction.
guardrails: Private rehearsal audio streaming is opt-in per session.
Do not persist raw audio by default.
Use push-to-talk or session-level consent for live rooms.

api_surfaces.name: Audio and transcription APIs
api_surfaces.use: Transcribe field notes, rehearsal talkback, or exported review bounces when the material is approved for upload.
guardrails: Prefer transcript files or short review excerpts over raw private audio.
Tag transcripts with source, consent, and retention notes.
Do not upload third-party copyrighted material unless rights permit that processing.

api_surfaces.name: Apps SDK and MCP
api_surfaces.use: Expose a future ChatGPT-facing project control surface for inventory lookup, worker brief rendering, rights checks, and DAW/account automation requests.
guardrails: Expose narrow tools rather than arbitrary shell, filesystem, browser, or DAW control.
Require explicit confirmation before account-gated downloads, installs, purchases, Live-set mutation, exports, or publishing.
Record only high-level action receipts and redacted evidence.

data_classes.notes: Default input for structured worker planning.
Inventory scripts must avoid copying licensed content or absolute home paths.
Approved audio binaries remain local or external; Git tracks provenance only.
Use local processing or transcript summaries unless a session-specific upload is approved.
Automation may use an already-authenticated browser/app session, but must not capture or persist secrets.
Install through official account flows and verify by inventory metadata only.

tool_contracts.output: Sanitized installed-library and DAW environment summary.
Pass/fail rights and provenance decision with unresolved risk notes.
Reviewable JSON patch proposal for Live/Max for Live implementation.
must_not: mutate a Live set without approval

tool_contracts.output: Redacted account-flow action receipt plus refreshed inventory delta.
Human-reviewable DAW action plan, Max for Live contract update, or local-only session receipt.
must_not: export or publish without release QA
log private performance content by default

tool_contracts.output: Release readiness checklist covering CI, credits, sources, installs, renders, and residual risks.
must_not: mark missing sample credits as acceptable

approval_gates.trigger: Download or transform a public-domain source file.
Send private rehearsal audio, lyrics, or unreleased take content to a cloud API.
Use an authenticated Ableton, Arturia, or other vendor account session.
Buy, license, activate, or materially change entitlement state for a vendor product.
required_evidence: price or license terms visible to user

approval_gates.trigger: Apply automation to an Ableton Live set, Max for Live device, controller mapping, or performance template.
Render, publish, distribute, or hand off production audio.

max_for_live_bridge.strategy: Use Max for Live and Live-session automation as local execution targets for reviewed proposals and receipts.
rollback_path: Keep all orchestration changes in Git branches.
Before mutating local DAW sessions, save a timestamped local copy outside Git.
Record the applied proposal id and affected tracks/devices.
Re-run inventory and repository validation after automation.
